channel.title: Nature
channel.description: Nature is the foremost international weekly scientific journal in the world and is the flagship journal for Nature Portfolio. It publishes the finest peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nature publishes landmark papers, award winning news, leading comment and expert opinion on important, topical scientific news and events that enable readers to share the latest discoveries in science and evolve the discussion amongst the global scientific community.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Not just a chip off the old block: nanoparticles reveal odd traits
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 05 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00323-3

Melting point of nanoscale sodium particles differs to that of bulk sodium.
item.dc:title: Not just a chip off the old block: nanoparticles reveal odd traits

item.title: Large-scale analogue quantum simulation using atom dot arrays
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10053-7

A new platform comprising large-scale 2D arrays of quantum dots patterned with sub-nanometre precision, with each quantum dot defined by tens of phosphorus atoms doped into silicon, allows for analogue simulation of quantum materials on arbitrary lattices.
item.dc:title: Large-scale analogue quantum simulation using atom dot arrays
item.dc:creator: M. B. Donnelly
Y. Chung
R. Garreis
S. Plugge
D. Pye
M. Kiczynski
J. Támara-Isaza

Y. L. Hsueh
A. M. Saffat-Ee Huq
C. R. Myers
R. Rahman
J. G. Keizer
S. K. Gorman
M. Y. Simmons

item.title: Discovery Learning predicts battery cycle life from minimal experiments
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09951-7

Discovery Learning, a machine learning approach integrating active learning, physics-guided learning and zero-shot learning into a human-like reasoning loop, is described for predicting the battery cycle life of new designs from minimal experiments, reducing prototyping requirements.
item.dc:title: Discovery Learning predicts battery cycle life from minimal experiments
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Zhang
Yifei Zhang
Baozhao Yi
Yao Ren
Qi Jiao
Hanyu Bai
Weiran Jiang
Ziyou Song

item.title: Signatures of fractional charges via anyon–trions in twisted MoTe<sub>2</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10101-w

Fractionally charged excitations at zero magnetic field in twisted MoTe2 bilayers, a recently discovered fractional quantum anomalous Hall system, are observed via anyon-trions, excitonic complexes formed by binding a trion to a fractional charge.
item.dc:title: Signatures of fractional charges via anyon–trions in twisted MoTe<sub>2</sub>
item.dc:creator: Weijie Li
Christiano Wang Beach
Chaowei Hu
Takashi Taniguchi
Kenji Watanabe
Jiun-Haw Chu
Ataç Imamoğlu
Ting Cao
Di Xiao
Xiaodong Xu

item.title: Imaging a terahertz superfluid plasmon in a two-dimensional superconductor
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10082-2

Terahertz microspectroscopic imaging at subgap millielectronvolt energies of a two-dimensional superfluid plasmon in few-layer Bi2Sr2CaCu2O8+x is demonstrated, allowing the spatial resolution of its deeply subdiffractive terahertz electrodynamics.
item.dc:title: Imaging a terahertz superfluid plasmon in a two-dimensional superconductor
item.dc:creator: A. von Hoegen
T. Tai
C. J. Allington
M. Yeung
J. Pettine
M. H. Michael
E. Viñas Boström
X. Cui
K. Torres
A. E. Kossak
B. Lee
G. S. D. Beach
G. D. Gu
A. Rubio
P. Kim
N. Gedik

item.title: Spin-wave band-pass filters for 6G communication
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10057-3

A spin-wave ladder architecture based on single-crystal yttrium iron garnet requiring only one external magnetic bias for manufacturing tunable compact filters using modern micromachining fabrication methods is described, demonstrating applications in future communications systems.
item.dc:title: Spin-wave band-pass filters for 6G communication
item.dc:creator: Connor Devitt
Sudhanshu Tiwari
Bill Zivasatienraj
Sunil A. Bhave

item.title: Resolving intervalley gaps and many-body resonances in moiré superconductors
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10067-1

This study of magic-angle twisted trilayer graphene moiré superconductors using scanning tunnelling microscopy and spectroscopy identifies two energy gaps that develop from many-body resonance in this highly tunable class of materials.
item.dc:title: Resolving intervalley gaps and many-body resonances in moiré superconductors
item.dc:creator: Hyunjin Kim
Gautam Rai
Lorenzo Crippa
Dumitru Călugăru
Haoyu Hu
Youngjoon Choi
Lingyuan Kong
Eli Baum

Ludwig Holleis
Kenji Watanabe
Takashi Taniguchi
Andrea F. Young
B. Andrei Bernevig
Roser Valentí
Giorgio Sangiovanni
Tim Wehling
Stevan Nadj-Perge

item.title: Machine learning slashes the testing needed to work out battery lifetimes
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00168-w

A machine-learning method that reasons and adapts has been developed to solve one of the most time-consuming bottlenecks in battery development.
item.dc:title: Machine learning slashes the testing needed to work out battery lifetimes
item.dc:creator: Chao Hu

item.title: Quantum computers will finally be useful: what’s behind the revolution
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 04 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00312-6

A string of surprising advances suggests usable quantum computers could be here in a decade.
item.dc:title: Quantum computers will finally be useful: what’s behind the revolution
item.dc:creator: Davide Castelvecchi
